ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.

translated: Jacob went to his father and said, "The women are menstruating, so I will go to Mahanam!"

ref: They kept on wrestling until the man said, “Let go of me! It's almost daylight.” “You can't go until you bless me,” Jacob replied.
text: Denii a baal ŋɔ, “Gil n kaali, bɔr chaanɔɔrɛ.” Ka Jekɔb ŋɔ, “Màá gil i kaali, di haŋ kissee lɛi.”
translated: Then he said, “Take your bows and quivers and go out into the fields to hunt.” And Jacob said, “You are my mother’s favorite, stay here."

ref: While his servants were digging in the valley, they found a spring-fed well.
text: U balaa ge faa kaa ki kɔlɔŋ bɛl a waluu biini di na niinanii.

ref: I sweated every day, and I couldn't sleep at night because of the cold.

ref: Abraham named that place “The Lord Will Provide.” And even now people say, “On the mountain of the Lord it will be provided.”

text: Denii Zilpa kaa lul biibaal tɛɛ Jekɔb.
translated: Denii and Zilpa are the two maids of Jacob

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Jekɔb n wa na baa u ŋɔ, “Kɔraawiizi ni u mɛlikazi chɔɔllɛ keŋ!” U faa dau de sɔŋ Mahanam.
translated: Jacob told his father, “The women are not giving you any more children!” Then he went to the town of Mahanam.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Awia, u ta newea, ba liu. U ta baabuu ba du u himmaa duula, di ɛɛ baal ni haaŋ.
translated: The dog, it has caught, a goat. It has caught a sheep and the owner of the sheep is not happy.

ref: Get some good lumber and build a boat. Put rooms in it and cover it with tar inside and out.
text: Awia, nyiŋŋi daananaa di kuari muugberzenzeŋ bɛl, í wer di ra zuu u biini lugɔ dia. Nyiŋŋi kotaal ham u biini ni u harbɔɔ maa nɛnnɛ.
translated: The elders, our ancestors, were very wise, that's why they said that a child's training starts from the womb. Our ancestors knew that a child and their upbringing are closely linked.

ref: I will give you a lot of descendants, and they will become great nations. Some of them will even be kings.
text: N daŋ tɛi nannakpila werge, baŋhina daŋ ɛɛ koorizenaa. I daŋ dɔɔ nannakpila werge, guŋ gil bá birgi hɛrkpila hina.
translated: When we go to the farm, we always return with something. If we go to the farm, we will bring back a lot of things.

ref: When they had another son,
text: Denii Zilpa kaa lul biibaal tɛɛ Jekɔb.
translated: Denii Zilpa said to her husband Jacob.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Jekɔb n wa na baa u ŋɔ, “Kɔraawiizi ni u mɛlikazi chɔɔllɛ keŋ!” U faa dau de sɔŋ Mahanam.
translated: Jacob told them, "The crocodiles have eaten my friend's farm!" He went to report to Mahanam.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Awia, u ta newea, ba liu. U ta baabuu ba du u himmaa duula, di ɛɛ baal ni haaŋ.
translated: The dog, it has caught a goat, and it is running. It has caught a sheep and is taking it to its owner, and they will give it a reward.

ref: Get some good lumber and build a boat. Put rooms in it and cover it with tar inside and out.
text: Awia, nyiŋŋi daananaa di kuari muugberzenzeŋ bɛl, í wer di ra zuu u biini lugɔ dia. Nyiŋŋi kotaal ham u biini ni u harbɔɔ maa nɛnnɛ.
translated: The child, having been given the corn dough, put it in the cooking pot, and when it was done, she took it out and gave it to her mother to eat. The child then went to her mother and gave her the food and the drink that was left.

ref: I will give you a lot of descendants, and they will become great nations. Some of them will even be kings.
text: N daŋ tɛi nannakpila werge, baŋhina daŋ ɛɛ koorizenaa. I daŋ dɔɔ nannakpila werge, guŋ gil bá birgi hɛrkpila hina.
translated: When the elders gather at the place of discussion, they sit in a circle. When they finish gathering at the place of discussion, they begin to share stories and experiences.

ref: When they had another son,
text: Denii Zilpa kaa lul biibaal tɛɛ Jekɔb.
translated: Denii is the wife of Jacob

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Jekɔb n wa na baa u ŋɔ, “Kɔraawiizi ni u mɛlikazi chɔɔllɛ keŋ!” U faa dau de sɔŋ Mahanam.
translated: Jacob said to them, "The Creator of the universe is the one who owns everything!" He then went to the town of Mahanam.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Awia, u ta newea, ba liu. U ta baabuu ba du u himmaa duula, di ɛɛ baal ni haaŋ.
translated: The dog, it is big, it is black. It is a male dog and it is very fierce, it does not like people.

ref: Get some good lumber and build a boat. Put rooms in it and cover it with tar inside and out.
text: Awia, nyiŋŋi daananaa di kuari muugberzenzeŋ bɛl, í wer di ra zuu u biini lugɔ dia. Nyiŋŋi kotaal ham u biini ni u harbɔɔ maa nɛnnɛ.
translated: The child, having gone to the farm to gather the harvest, saw a snake in the farm and ran back home. The child told the mother and the father about it immediately.

ref: I will give you a lot of descendants, and they will become great nations. Some of them will even be kings.
text: N daŋ tɛi nannakpila werge, baŋhina daŋ ɛɛ koorizenaa. I daŋ dɔɔ nannakpila werge, guŋ gil bá birgi hɛrkpila hina.
translated: When we go to the farm, we always return with something. If we go to the farm, we will return with something to show for it.

ref: When they had another son,
text: Denii Zilpa kaa lul biibaal tɛɛ Jekɔb.
translated: Denii and Zilpa are Jacob's wives.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Awia, u ta newea, ba liu. U ta baabuu ba du u himmaa duula, di ɛɛ baal ni haaŋ.
translated: The child, he is crying, the dog is barking. He is holding the dog and the dog is licking his face, and that is why the child is laughing.

ref: Get some good lumber and build a boat. Put rooms in it and cover it with tar inside and out.
text: Awia, nyiŋŋi daananaa di kuari muugberzenzeŋ bɛl, í wer di ra zuu u biini lugɔ dia. Nyiŋŋi kotaal ham u biini ni u harbɔɔ maa nɛnnɛ.
translated: The elders, our ancestors were very powerful people, they could do anything they wanted. Our ancestors were rich and had many children and grandchildren.

ref: I will give you a lot of descendants, and they will become great nations. Some of them will even be kings.

translated: When the hunters go to hunt, they go with one intention. If the hunters go to hunt, they must first make a sacrifice to the spirits of the forest.

ref: When they had another son,

translated: The elders, they have a proverb that says if you are going to follow a path, you should follow it to its end. The elders also say that if you are going to do something, you should do it well or not do it at all.
